results.editorial:
PALS Caring Home sits at No. 19, Jalan 32, Kampung Cheras Baru — a residential neighbourhood in southern Cheras that straddles the Selangor-KL border. The facility markets itself as a nursing home providing high-quality assisted living, and its website lists a wide range of conditions it supports: stroke, dementia, Parkinson's, diabetes, cancer, palliative, post-hospitalization, post-surgery, and arthritis care. With a Google rating of 4.9 from 11 reviews, the limited review pool suggests a small, community-facing operation rather than a large corporate facility.

The website indicates that care plans are personalised to each resident and that stay-in caregivers provide overnight coverage when needed. Listed programmes include daily exercises, nutritious meals designed with seniors' digestive needs in mind, and various activities for physical and mental engagement. PALS also promotes home-care services alongside its residential offering, which suggests staffing capacity to support both settings. Dementia care and rehabilitation therapy are specifically cited as areas of focus, though the depth of clinical capability — specialist nurses, physiotherapy equipment, or dementia unit infrastructure — is not detailed on the site.

Bed count and pricing are not publicly disclosed. The Cheras Baru location puts residents reasonably close to Hospital Cheras and the range of specialist facilities along the MRR2 corridor, which matters for follow-up appointments. Families should ask directly about nurse-to-resident ratios on night shifts, what constitutes dementia care in practice (secure unit versus general supervision), and the precise scope of rehabilitation — whether a physiotherapist attends in person or exercises are caregiver-led.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Dementia care, Rehabilitation, Palliative, Respite, Post-hospitalization
facts.red_flags: No bed count or pricing disclosed; review count very low (11); dementia and rehab care claimed but clinical depth unverified

results.editorial:
Glory Living Care Home is positioned in the Taman Sri Ukay / Taman Hillview area of Ampang — a hillside residential enclave off Jalan Ulu Kelang, about 15 minutes from the Ampang town centre. The facility describes itself as a retirement home and assisted living centre focused on seniors who value independence, friendship, and a healthy lifestyle, with an emphasis on a clean, hygienic, and home-like environment suited to those who need support but wish to remain socially engaged. Contact is via a mobile number (+60 10-962 8683), and the facility has a Facebook page but no independent website with detailed service listings.

Based on its Google listing category of aged care, assisted living, and retirement home, the facility appears to cater to relatively mobile or semi-independent residents rather than high-dependency nursing cases. The Sri Ukay hillside setting offers a quieter, greener environment compared to urban care homes, which some families find preferable for residents with mild cognitive decline or those in earlier stages of frailty. The 4.8 Google rating across 11 reviews is positive, though the small sample limits confidence. No bed count, pricing, or staffing details are publicly available.

With an online footprint this limited, families should visit in person before committing. Key questions: Is registered nursing staff present around the clock, or is this primarily caregiver-staffed? What conditions can the home manage — incontinence, tube feeding, wound care — versus what would trigger a transfer? The Ampang area has reasonable hospital access via Hospital Ampang and several private facilities along the Ampang-KL corridor, but confirm the home's emergency protocol and hospital relationships before placement.

facts.care_types: Aged care, Assisted living, Retirement home
facts.red_flags: No website with service details; no bed count or pricing disclosed; care capability limits unclear; review count very low (11)

results.editorial:
Noble Care Nursing Home Klang occupies a shophouse unit at 46-B, Lorong Baling, Kawasan 18, in the older industrial-residential fringe of Klang town. Established in 2012 according to the operator's website, it is one branch of a multi-site Noble Care group operating across the Klang Valley. The Klang branch is positioned as a full nursing home handling complex care, not just general aged accommodation, with round-the-clock nursing coverage and what the website describes as personalized health and wellness programmes within a safe, comfortable living environment.

The Noble Care website lists a notable range of clinical procedures: NG tube changing, Foley's catheter care, colostomy bag management, bedsore dressing, tracheostomy care, post-operative care, and cerebral palsy care — a breadth that indicates nursing staff are present and trained for dependency management beyond basic assistance. Dementia care and physiotherapy are also listed. Respite and palliative care are offered as distinct service categories. The shophouse format typical of Kawasan 18 means the physical footprint will be modest; families should inspect room sizes and outdoor access during any site visit. Bed count is not publicly stated.

Klang's Kawasan 18 is close to Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah (HTAR) — the main government referral hospital for the district — which is practical for specialist follow-ups. Pricing is not disclosed online; contact is via +60 16-278 6993. Ask about actual nurse-to-resident ratios, licence number, and whether any of the listed clinical procedures require prior arrangement or are routinely available on-site. The Google rating of 4.6 from 11 reviews is modest but positive.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Dementia care, Respite, Palliative, Post-operative, Rehabilitation
facts.red_flags: Shophouse setting limits space; bed count undisclosed; pricing not published; review count low (11)

results.editorial:
Gam Yam Senior Home operates from 327, Jalan 16, Kampung Baru Ampang — a long-established Chinese residential neighbourhood in Ampang that sits on the Selangor-KL boundary. The facility's Google listing categorises it as a retirement home, assisted living facility, and home health care service, suggesting it spans both residential and outreach care. A directory listing places physiotherapy and residential care for the elderly and disabled among its stated activities. The address is shared or closely associated with another entity listed as AIM Healthcare at the same premises, which warrants clarification on who operates the facility and under what licence.

With a Google rating of 3.3 from 11 reviews — the lowest in this batch — Gam Yam has a materially weaker satisfaction signal than neighbouring facilities in the Ampang area. No website or Facebook presence was found, and no details about capacity, staffing, pricing, or the scope of nursing care are publicly available. The data field for languages listed as '50' in the source data appears to be a data entry error rather than a meaningful value. The Kampung Baru Ampang setting is a densely built residential area with limited outdoor space, though Hospital Ampang is within a few kilometres.

The combination of a below-average rating, absence of any public-facing information, and an unresolved address overlap with another entity means this home warrants careful due diligence before considering placement. Families should request the facility's Care Centre Act licence number, ask about the relationship to AIM Healthcare at the same address, confirm which conditions are managed versus referred out, and seek references from current families. A visit during a mealtime or activity period will give a clearer picture of day-to-day conditions than a formal tour.

facts.care_types: Retirement home, Assisted living, Home health care
facts.red_flags: Low Google rating (3.3/5); no website or Facebook; possible address overlap with AIM Healthcare; languages field in source data appears corrupt; no capacity or pricing data

results.editorial:
The Senior Care operates two licensed branches in the heart of Petaling Jaya: a 21-bed flagship at No. 2, Jalan 12/13, Section 12, and a 16-bed quieter facility at No. 35a, Jalan Bukit 11/2, Section 11. Both are purpose-built with step-free access and toilets throughout — a practical detail that matters for residents with limited mobility or fall risk. The Section 12 site is described as vibrant and socially oriented, while Section 11 is positioned as a calmer, more intimate setting approximately 700 metres from University of Malaya Medical Centre (PPUM), which also happens to be close to Assunta, Columbia Asia, and Beacon hospitals.

The Senior Care's website lists a considered clinical offer: long-term nursing care, assisted living, dementia care, respite, rehabilitation, day care, wound care management, and physiotherapy — delivered through qualified nurses and physiotherapists described as companions as much as clinicians. Six freshly cooked meals daily, CCTV coverage in rooms and common areas, in-house doctor, weekly physiotherapist visits, a landscaped garden at the Section 11 branch, and a Koi pond and patio suggest an operator that has thought about resident experience. Section 11 has WiFi and 24-hour surveillance. The facility is registered with AGECOPE, the industry association for residential aged care operators.

With a 4.9 Google rating from 10 reviews, both branches appear well-regarded, though the review count is small. Pricing is not published; contact is via +60 12-667 6125. Key questions for families: what is the Section 12 outdoor access arrangement (urban shophouse areas can limit this), how are dementia residents managed relative to the general population, and is the in-house doctor a GP or a geriatrician? The combined 37 beds across two branches means waiting lists are plausible — confirm availability and expected timelines early.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living, Dementia care, Respite, Rehabilitation, Day care, Wound care
facts.red_flags: Pricing not disclosed; small bed count (37 total across 2 branches) may mean waiting lists; Section 12 outdoor space uncertain

results.editorial:
Well Living Care Centre Sdn. Bhd. is a licensed, 80-bed nursing home and retirement home located at Lot 4832, Lorong 7, Kampung Sungai Terentang, in the Rawang area of northern Selangor — a hillside rural-residential setting that its AGECOPE listing describes simply as 'a home on the hill.' The Rawang location, while further from central KL than most facilities in this batch, offers quieter surroundings and typically lower pricing compared to urban Klang Valley homes. It is registered with AGECOPE, the industry association for residential care operators, and is classified as a non-NGO commercial facility. Contact is through a mobile number (+60 17-577 5730), consistent with the Kampung Kenanga area listed on the Google profile.

At 80 beds, Well Living is among the larger independent homes in the Rawang corridor. A Facebook page exists under 'Well Living' in Rawang, indicating some community presence, though the page itself could not be accessed to extract programme details. No clinical capability breakdown, pricing, staffing ratios, or programme details are publicly available beyond the basic directory listing. The facility carries a 4.6 Google rating from 10 reviews, consistent with adequate care without standout commendation.

For families considering Rawang, the key practical considerations are proximity to medical services — Hospital Selayang is the nearest tertiary government hospital, approximately 25–30 minutes away, while private options in the area are limited — and transport access for family visits. The capacity of 80 beds suggests the operator has some infrastructure, but the rural setting and sparse online presence mean an in-person visit is especially important. Ask about nursing coverage overnight, which conditions the home can manage versus refer, and what the transfer protocol is in an emergency.

facts.red_flags: Limited online presence; no clinical detail or pricing disclosed; Rawang location means longer hospital transfer times

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Nur Hasanah (PJNH) is a specialised transit home for displaced male patients in Beranang, southern Selangor — a distinctly different model from private nursing homes. Established in September 2006 and operated under the Care Centre Act 1993, the facility sits at Lot 842, Lorong Dato' Dagang Haji Tahir, Kampung Sesapan Kelubi, 43700 Beranang. It does not serve private-paying families in the conventional sense: admission is through hospital Medical Social Work Departments, and the population served is male patients without family support who have been discharged or are awaiting permanent placement by the Department of Social Welfare (JKM). Residents include the elderly, persons with disabilities, new Muslims (muallaf), and those otherwise classified as destitute. The listed bed capacity is 30, and since founding, more than 220 residents have passed through.

The care model centres on stabilisation and transitional support rather than long-term residency. Residents typically stay up to six months while social workers work to secure permanent placement at government homes such as Rumah Seri Kenangan or Rumah Ehsan. Programmes include counselling, spiritual and religious activities, recreation, and medical care with transport assistance. The facility is financially sustained through individual, corporate, and government donations, which are eligible for income tax deduction under Section 44(6) of the Income Tax Act — a sign of its formalised charitable status.

Families with a private-pay budget or a relative requiring standard nursing home placement will find PJNH outside the typical options they are comparing. This facility exists to address a specific welfare gap for patients with no family or financial resources. Those who are eligible — and whose loved one meets the criteria — must route applications through hospital social workers, not direct contact with the facility. Confirm the current referral process and available capacity with PJNH directly at 03-87668100, as transit demand from hospitals fluctuates.

facts.care_types: Transit home, Welfare care (male destitute patients, elderly, PWD, muallaf)
facts.religion: Islamic orientation (spiritual programmes for muallaf); appears Muslim-run
facts.red_flags: Not a private-pay facility; admission through hospital social workers only; male residents only; maximum 6-month stay; not suitable for typical family nursing home search

results.editorial:
St. Mary's Nursing Home is among the more established brands in the Klang Valley's private aged care sector, with five branches across KL and Petaling Jaya. The branch listed here at Taman Halimahton is at 2A, Jalan Halimahton, off the 3rd Mile Old Klang Road — a quiet residential pocket between the busy Old Klang Road and the Mid Valley/Bangsar corridor. The group was founded approximately 30 years ago by Madam Jagit Kour (known as Madam Sonia), who brought nursing training from Singapore and the United Kingdom. A second branch exists at 15C, Jalan Penghulu Mat, also in Taman Halimahton. The Google rating for the Taman Halimahton location is 4.4 from 10 reviews.

St. Mary's lists its services as elderly day programmes, private nursing care, assisted living, short-term residential care, and supplementary services tailored to individual needs. The group's emphasis on prayer sessions accommodating residents' own religious denominations, dietician-recommended meals, and daily wellness activities suggests an established routine rather than a bare-minimum operation. With five branches, the organisation has staffing depth and operational experience — reflected in the fact that the PJ branches reportedly operate with waiting lists. Bed count and pricing for the Taman Halimahton branch are not published.

The Old Klang Road location offers good access to Assunta Hospital, KPJ Damansara, and Pantai Hospital — all within 10–15 minutes. Families should clarify which specific St. Mary's entity operates this address (St. Mary's Aged Home Sdn Bhd and St. Mary's Elderly Home appear to be related but distinct registrations), and confirm the current contact person, as the group has expanded and management contacts have evolved. Ask about nurse qualification levels, how clinical escalation is handled at night, and whether the multi-faith prayer provision extends to the resident's specific religious background.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living, Day care, Respite, Short-term residential
facts.religion: Multi-faith (prayer sessions for residents' own religion)
facts.red_flags: Multiple related entities with slightly different names may cause confusion; bed count and pricing not disclosed; review count low (10)

results.editorial:
De Luxe Retirement Home is a family-run operator with over 25 years in service, founded in 2000 by Susan Lim and co-founded with Dr. Kay Chee Goh — a geriatric practitioner with 40-plus years of experience who serves as the in-house doctor. The House 39 branch referred to here corresponds to the Kampung Tunku location at 37–39, Jalan SS 1/2, one of three licensed branches (the others at Jalan SS 1/1 Kampung Tunku and Jalan SS 3/39 Taman Universiti). The operator publishes its website at deluxerh.com and is fully licensed, with approvals from fire safety, Ministry of Health, Ministry of Social Welfare, and Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ).

De Luxe's website details a meaningful clinical capability: skilled nursing care for bedridden and stroke patients, medication management, wound care, NG-tube and PEG-tube feeding, patient lifting assistance, dementia and Alzheimer's care, Parkinson's rehabilitation, and daily assisted exercises. Physiotherapy and acupuncture are available on demand. Room options run from private air-conditioned rooms with ensuite bathrooms to more affordable shared rooms. Meals are described as nutritious Chinese home-cooked, which gives a sense of the cultural orientation. General Manager Alyssa Lim has aged care experience spanning Malaysia, Singapore, and Australia, suggesting the management has familiarity with international care standards. A 4.9 Google rating from 9 reviews is positive but based on a small sample.

With three branches in SS1 and SS3, the Kampung Tunku area is well-served by the De Luxe network, and the proximity to major PJ hospitals (Assunta, Columbia Asia, Beacon) is a practical advantage. Pricing is not published but the operator accepts WhatsApp inquiries at +60 12-291 3336. The listed categories include adult day care, which makes it a potential option for families who need daytime support without full residential placement. Ask specifically about the Kampung Tunku House 39 bed count, which rooms are in that branch versus the adjacent properties, and wait-list position if immediate placement is needed.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living, Dementia care, Parkinson's rehabilitation, Day care, Respite
facts.languages: Chinese (home-cooked meals indicate cultural orientation; likely Mandarin/Cantonese spoken)
facts.licence_number: Licensed by MOH, JKMM, MBPJ, and JBPM (fire dept); listed on deluxerh.com
facts.red_flags: Small review count (9); specific bed count per branch not published; three branches in close proximity may cause confusion about which 'House 39' refers to

results.editorial:
Oasis Nursing Home (安康之家) is located at No. 50, Jalan Sungai Kapar Indah 5E, Taman Sungai Kapar Indah, in the Kapar district of Klang — a suburban residential area north of Klang town proper. The facility is distinctive in that its founder and doctor-in-charge is Dr. Wendy Oon, a named physician whose involvement is documented on the facility's own website. The care team listed includes a head of nursing (Shaziq), an assistant head nurse (Siti Ramlah), and an in-house physiotherapist (Nur Ain) — a staff configuration that goes beyond what most small nursing homes publish, and indicates genuine clinical structure rather than caregiver-only operations. The facility has a Wix-hosted website and a Facebook page, and carries a perfect 5.0 Google rating from 8 reviews.

Oasis lists its care scope as: long-term care, short-term stays, post-hospitalization, stroke rehabilitation, post-operation, palliative care, and dementia care. A testimonial cited on the website describes improvement in a resident's bedsore from grade 4 to grade 1 — a clinically meaningful outcome that, if verified, suggests nursing wound care competency. The Chinese-language name (安康之家, literally 'home of health and tranquility') suggests the facility is oriented toward the Chinese-speaking community, consistent with the Kapar area's demographics, though the team includes Malay staff names indicating a mixed-language environment. Operating hours listed are Sunday through Friday, 8am–6pm, which refers to visiting or administrative hours; residential care is 24-hour.

Bed count is not disclosed. Kapar is roughly 15–20 minutes from Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah in Klang town, the main government hospital for the area, and 10 minutes from the Kapar town centre. With pricing not published, families should contact via +60 12-270 3166 or oasisnursingcare@gmail.com. Ask about the frequency of Dr. Oon's on-site presence versus on-call arrangement, what physiotherapy sessions look like in practice, and whether palliative care extends to end-of-life support or is primarily post-acute.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Stroke rehabilitation, Dementia care, Palliative, Post-hospitalization, Respite
facts.languages: Chinese (likely Mandarin/Cantonese given Chinese name and Kapar demographics); mixed Malay staff
facts.red_flags: Bed count undisclosed; very small review count (8) despite perfect rating; visiting hours Sunday–Friday only may limit family access on Saturdays
